channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
item.description: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools

item.title: [Interconnects] How open model ecosystems compound
item.description: How open model ecosystems compound

item.title: [Hacker News] Why senior developers fail to communicate their expertise
item.description: Why senior developers fail to communicate their expertise

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/mattpocock — Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
item.description: mattpocock's GitHub repository shares real-world engineering skills and resources from a .claude directory.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Thinking Machines' Native Interaction Models - TML-Interaction-Small 276B-A12B - advances SOTA Realtime Voice and kills standard VAD
item.description: [AINews] Thinking Machines' Native Interaction Models - TML-Interaction-Small 276B-A12B - advances SOTA Realtime Voice and kills standard VAD

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reinforcement learning for inverse structural design and rapid laser cutting of kirigami prototypes
item.description: RL-based inverse design framework generates compatible laser-cut patterns for shape-programmable metamaterial structures with 94.91% accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Path-Based Gradient Boosting for Graph-Level Prediction
item.description: PathBoost applies gradient boosting to graph-level classification/regression using path-based features, matching or outperforming graph neural networks on benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Distributional Reinforcement Learning via the Cram\'er Distance
item.description: Cramér-based Distributional Soft Actor-Critic (C-DSAC) outperforms baseline methods on robotic benchmarks through confidence-driven Q-value updates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Geometry-free prediction of inertial lift forces in microfluidic devices using deep learning
item.description: Deep learning model predicts inertial lift forces in microfluidic devices without explicit geometric parameters, generalizing across unseen channel designs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] BaLoRA: Bayesian Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Scale Models
item.description: BaLoRA introduces Bayesian uncertainty quantification to LoRA fine-tuning, improving accuracy closer to full fine-tuning across language, vision, and materials tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TTCD:Transformer Integrated Temporal Causal Discovery from Non-Stationary Time Series Data
item.description: TTCD discovers causal relationships in non-stationary time series using transformers with reconstruction-guided signal distillation to mitigate noise and spurious correlations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Do Foundation Model Embeddings Improve Cross-Country Crop Yield Generalisation? A Leave-One-Country-Out Evaluation in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.description: Foundation model embeddings provide no meaningful advantage over spectral features for cross-country maize yield prediction in sub-Saharan Africa.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Statistical Inference and Quality Measures of KV Cache Quantisations Inspired by TurboQuant
item.description: Comparative statistical analysis of three KV cache quantization schemes identifies KQV as optimal at 4-bit budgets, with bit-dependent performance trade-offs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Safety-Aware Denoiser for Text Diffusion Models
item.description: Safety-Aware Denoiser framework reduces unsafe text generation in diffusion models via inference-time guidance without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Feature Repulsion and Spectral Lock-in: An Empirical Study of Two-Layer Network Grokking
item.description: Research validates repulsion theorem for grokking features but finds spectral signature depends on activation function.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Block-Wise Differentiable Sinkhorn Attention: Tail-Refinement Gradients with a Gap-Aware Dustbin Bridge
item.description: Researchers present efficient block-wise Sinkhorn attention for long-context processing on TPU hardware using tail-refinement gradients.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Universal Gene Regulatory Network Inference: Unlocking Generalizable Regulatory Knowledge in Single-cell Foundation Models
item.description: Novel methods extract regulatory knowledge from single-cell foundation models to improve gene regulatory network inference on unseen genes and datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Customized Multimodal Role-Play
item.description: Researchers develop UniCharacter framework enabling multimodal models to generate consistent text and images for customized character personas from few-shot examples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Additive Atomic Forests for Symbolic Function and Antiderivative Discovery
item.description: Framework discovers symbolic functions and their antiderivatives from data using derivative algebra and atomic forests.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Interactive Inverse Reinforcement Learning of Interaction Scenarios via Bi-level Optimization
item.description: BISIRL uses bi-level optimization to enable active learner-expert interaction for inferring expert reward functions in interactive inverse reinforcement learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DARE: Diffusion Language Model Activation Reuse for Efficient Inference
item.description: DARE reuses redundant attention activations in diffusion LLMs to reduce per-layer latency by 1.20x with minimal performance degradation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Dendritic Neural Networks with Equilibrium Propagation
item.description: Dendritic structures enhance biologically plausible equilibrium propagation, improving performance on challenging datasets compared to standard methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Weight Pruning Amplifies Bias: A Multi-Method Study of Compressed LLMs for Edge AI
item.description: Activation-aware weight pruning amplifies bias in compressed LLMs, increasing stereotype reliance up to 84% while preserving language model quality.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DataArc-SynData-Toolkit: A Unified Closed-Loop Framework for Multi-Path, Multimodal, and Multilingual Data Synthesis
item.description: DataArc-SynData-Toolkit is an open-source framework for multimodal synthetic data generation with configuration-driven pipelines and unified quality controls.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reasoning emerges from constrained inference manifolds in large language models
item.description: LLM reasoning emerges within geometrically constrained low-dimensional manifolds requiring adequate expressivity and preserved information volume.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] HoReN: Normalized Hopfield Retrieval for Large-Scale Sequential Model Editing
item.description: HoReN enables scalable LLM editing through a Hopfield-based codebook approach that preserves existing knowledge while incorporating targeted updates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] NoiseRater: Meta-Learned Noise Valuation for Diffusion Model Training
item.description: NoiseRater uses meta-learning to assign importance scores to noise samples during diffusion model training, improving efficiency and generation quality.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Feature Rivalry in Sparse Autoencoder Representations: A Mechanistic Study of Uncertainty-Driven Feature Competition in LLMs
item.description: Researchers find negatively correlated SAE features (Feature Rivalry) correlate with model uncertainty and help predict language model answer correctness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A PyTorch Library of Turing-Complete Neural Networks
item.description: PyTorch package that compiles neural networks from Turing machine descriptions, producing models that exactly simulate specified machines without training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Temporal-Decay Shapley: A Time-Aware Data Valuation Framework for Time-Series Data
item.description: Temporal-Decay Shapley method improves data valuation for time-series ML by incorporating temporal decay weights and multi-scale fusion for noise detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quantitative Sobolev Approximation Bounds for Neural Operators with Empirical Validation on Burgers Equation
item.description: Researchers prove neural operators can uniformly approximate nonlinear operators in Sobolev norms with empirical validation on Burgers equation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Communication Dynamics Neural Networks: FFT-Diagonalized Layers for Improved Hessian Conditioning at Reduced Parameter Count
item.description: FFT-diagonalized neural network layers reduce parameters 3.8x with improved Hessian conditioning and minimal accuracy loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CERSA: Cumulative Energy-Retaining Subspace Adaptation for Memory-Efficient Fine-Tuning
item.description: CERSA uses SVD-based subspace adaptation for memory-efficient fine-tuning, outperforming LoRA with lower memory requirements across multiple domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Physics-Modeled Neural Networks
item.description: Physics-modeled neural networks replace activation functions with dynamical systems, achieving competitive performance while using fewer trainable parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Echo-LoRA: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning via Cross-Layer Representation Injection
item.description: Echo-LoRA improves LoRA fine-tuning by injecting cross-layer representations into shallow modules, boosting reasoning benchmarks without inference-time cost.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Learning Agent Routing From Early Experience
item.description: BoundaryRouter uses early behavioral experience to route queries between LLM inference and agent execution, reducing inference time 60.6% while improving performance 28.6%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Text Uncanny Valley: Non-Monotonic Performance Degradation in LLM Information Retrieval
item.description: LLMs exhibit non-monotonic performance degradation on corrupted text, with accuracy following a U-shaped curve as word-boundary corruption increases.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PSK@EEUCA 2026: Fine-Tuning Large Language Models with Synthetic Data Augmentation for Multi-Class Toxicity Detection in Gaming Chat
item.description: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B with 5% synthetic data augmentation achieved 0.6234 F1-macro on six-class toxicity detection for gaming chat messages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hallucination Detection via Activations of Open-Weight Proxy Analyzers
item.description: Proxy-analyzer framework using small open-weight models detects LLM hallucinations via internal activations, outperforming existing methods across seven architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reformulating KV Cache Eviction Problem for Long-Context LLM Inference
item.description: LaProx reformulates KV cache eviction as an output-aware problem, maintaining long-context LLM performance with only 5% of the cache.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Teaching Language Models to Think in Code
item.description: ThinC framework uses code as the primary reasoner for mathematical problem-solving instead of interleaved natural language, with 4B model outperforming larger baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SpecBlock: Block-Iterative Speculative Decoding with Dynamic Tree Drafting
item.description: SpecBlock proposes block-iterative speculative decoding combining path dependence with efficient parallel drafting using dynamic tree allocation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PaT: Planning-after-Trial for Efficient Test-Time Code Generation
item.description: Planning-after-Trial adaptively invokes planning only upon verification failure in code generation, reducing inference costs by 69% while maintaining model performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From 0-Order Selection to 2-Order Judgment: Combinatorial Hardening Exposes Compositional Failures in Frontier LLMs
item.description: LogiHard transforms multiple-choice questions into harder logical reasoning tasks, revealing compositional failures in frontier LLMs with 31-56% accuracy degradation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MIPIAD: Multilingual Indirect Prompt Injection Attack Defense with Qwen -- TF-IDF Hybrid and Meta-Ensemble Learning
item.description: MIPIAD framework defends against multilingual indirect prompt injection attacks using Qwen-based neural classifier, TF-IDF features, and ensemble methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Understanding Performance Collapse in Layer-Pruned Large Language Models via Decision Representation Transitions
item.description: Pruning LLM layers causes sudden performance collapse by disrupting the Silent Phase, which enables critical decision representation transitions for correct predictions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MedAction: Towards Active Multi-turn Clinical Diagnostic LLMs
item.description: MedAction enables multi-turn clinical diagnosis in LLMs using 32K synthetic diagnostic trajectories to improve test ordering and medical reasoning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Rethinking Dense Sequential Chains: Reasoning Language Models Can Extract Answers from Sparse, Order-Shuffling Chain-of-Thoughts
item.description: Reasoning language models extract answers from shuffled, sparse chain-of-thought if numeric values are preserved.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LaTER: Efficient Test-Time Reasoning via Latent Exploration and Explicit Verification
item.description: LaTER uses latent space exploration followed by explicit reasoning to reduce inference token usage by 16-32% while maintaining accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Activation Differences Reveal Backdoors: A Comparison of SAE Architectures
item.description: Differential SAEs substantially outperform Crosscoders for detecting backdoors in language models by revealing directional activation shifts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Mean-Pooled Cosine Similarity is Not Length-Invariant: Theory and Cross-Domain Evidence for a Length-Invariant Alternative
item.description: Mean-pooled cosine similarity exhibits length bias in neural representations; researchers propose Centered Kernel Alignment as a length-invariant alternative.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Gradient-Based LoRA Rank Allocation Under GRPO: An Empirical Study
item.description: Gradient-based LoRA rank allocation transfers poorly from supervised fine-tuning to GRPO reinforcement learning due to flatter gradient landscapes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Proxy Presumption: From Semantic Embeddings to Valid Social Measures
item.description: A framework validates semantic embeddings for measuring social constructs by addressing confounding factors through counterfactual neutralization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Generating training datasets for legal chatbots in Korean
item.description: Researchers generate 700 million labeled utterances using local grammar graphs to train LIGA, a Korean legal chatbot improving justice access.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SSP-based construction of evaluation-annotated data for fine-grained aspect-based sentiment analysis
item.description: Researchers created a Korean e-commerce ABSA corpus with fine-grained aspect-value annotations, achieving F1 scores of 0.88-0.90 with BERT models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Data Contamination in Neural Hieroglyphic Translation: A Reproducibility Study
item.description: Data contamination in hieroglyphic translation benchmark inflates BLEU scores significantly; decontaminated test set reveals true performance of neural translation for endangered languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GRaSp: Automatic Example Optimization for In-Context Learning in Low-Data Tasks
item.description: GRaSp uses genetic algorithms to automatically optimize in-context examples for language models, improving performance on low-data NER tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Think-with-Rubrics: From External Evaluator to Internal Reasoning Guidance
item.description: Think-with-Rubrics integrates rubric generation into LLM reasoning as internal guidance, improving instruction-following performance by 3.87 points over external evaluation baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Moltbook Files: A Harmless Slopocalypse or Humanity's Last Experiment
item.description: Researchers release Moltbook dataset of AI agent forum activity, finding fine-tuning on this data substantially reduces language model truthfulness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SEIF: Self-Evolving Reinforcement Learning for Instruction Following
item.description: SEIF proposes a self-evolving framework for improving LLMs' instruction-following through co-evolving instruction difficulty and model capability.
